bibliographic_data.title: EllSeg: An Ellipse Segmentation Framework for Robust Gaze Tracking
bibliographic_data.abstract: Ellipse fitting, an essential component in pupil or iris tracking based video oculography, is performed on previously segmented eye parts generated using various computer vision techniques. Several factors, such as occlusions due to eyelid shape, camera position or eyelashes, frequently break ellipse fitting algorithms that rely on well-defined pupil or iris edge segments. In this work, we propose training a convolutional neural network to directly segment entire elliptical structures and demonstrate that such a framework is robust to occlusions and offers superior pupil and iris tracking performance (at least 10% and 24% increase in pupil and iris center detection rate respectively within a two-pixel error margin) compared to using standard eye parts segmentation for multiple publicly available synthetic segmentation datasets.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes EllSeg, a framework that can be utilized with any encoder-decoder architecture for pupil and iris ellipse segmentation, enabling prediction of the pupil and iris as full elliptical structures despite the presence of occlusions.
core_content.key_methodology: A convolutional neural network is trained to predict entire elliptical eye regions (the full pupil and the full iris) along with the remaining background, instead of standard visible eye-parts segmentation. The framework is tested using three network architectures: a modified Dense Fully Connected Network (DenseElNet), RITnet, and Deep-VOG.
outcomes: The proposed EllSeg framework offers superior pupil and iris tracking performance compared to traditional methods.
It can be utilized with any encoder-decoder architecture for ellipse segmentation.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Ellipse fitting is a critical component in video oculography, particularly for gaze tracking. However, occlusions due to eyelid shape, camera position, or eyelashes often break existing algorithms that rely on well-defined pupil or iris edge segments. This paper introduces EllSeg, an ellipse segmentation framework designed to handle such occlusions by training convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to predict entire elliptical structures of the eye, including both the full pupil and the full iris. The authors demonstrate that this approach significantly improves gaze tracking performance, with a 10% increase in pupil center detection rate and a 24% increase in iris center detection rate within a two-pixel error margin compared to traditional methods. The framework is tested using three network architectures: DenseElNet, RITnet, and Deep-VOG, on multiple publicly available datasets.
